

![http://www.transitioning.org/wp-content/uploads/2012/05/SMU\_logo.jpg][image1]

| IS213 Enterprise Solution Development Lab 7 – LGB Bank Case Study |
| :---: |

Powered by

**Prerequisite**

* You must have OutSystems Studio installed on your machine.  
* You must have an OutSystems account to your personal cloud environment.

(See Lab 3.1 OutSystems Lab Environment Setup Guide)

**Learning Objectives**

In this lab, you will learn how to create a composite service using OutSystems.  The composite service will invoke a few atomic services which are already deployed in SMU’s enterprise cloud environment.  Altogether, the solution will implement the LGB mobile app discussed in the LGB Bank Case Study lecture slides.

**Activities**:

You will be performing the following main functions in the OutSystems environment.

* Create a composite service called IssueInsurancePolicy with a REST API endpoint.  The REST API will include 1 method:  
  * IssueInsurancePolicy  
* You will use Postman to test your REST API.  
* You will use a ready-made mobile app to test your API as part of the total solution.

**Tasks**:

Task 1 – Use a mobile app to sign up for an account with LGB Bank

Task 2 – Create a new Module in your personal environment

Task 3 – Setup Site Properties

Task 4 – Consume REST APIs

Task 5 – Setup Data Structures

Task 6 – Add Request/Reply Handlers

Task 7 – Add Server Action – PublishPolicyEvent

Task 8 – Expose REST API – IssueInsurancePolicy

Task 9 – Test completed solution using the mobile app

Task 10 – Extra Stuff

**Troubleshooting Guide**

**Lab Overview:**

You will develop the “Issue Insurance Policy” composite service in your personal OutSystems cloud environment.  All the other components shown below, including the mobile app, are already built using OutSystems and running in SMU’s enterprise cloud environment.

**![A diagram of a businessDescription automatically generated][image2]**

Your “Issue Insurance Policy” composite service will orchestrate the following sequence:

1. Create Insurance Account		\- Atomic Service  
2. Make Payment 			\- Composite Service  
3. Create Insurance Policy		\- Atomic Service  
4. Update Insurance Account		\- Atomic Service  
5. Publish Policy Event 		\- Publish message via Rabbit MQ  
   

Publishing the Policy Event via a Topic Exchange:

![A diagram of payment and paymentDescription automatically generated][image3]

You will use the mobile app to issue/renew insurance policies (Travel, Home and Auto).

You will receive SMS and Email messages for Payment Events or Policy Events.

Task 1	Use Mobile App to sign up for an LGB Bank account

1. Scan the below QR Code.

Add the app to your Home Screen.

![A screenshot of a computer errorDescription automatically generated][image4]

Or click the link to the app:  
[https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/LGB\_MobileApp/Login](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/LGB_MobileApp/Login) 

What is a PWA app?

A progressive web application (PWA), or progressive web app, is a type of web app that can be installed on a device as a standalone application. PWAs are installed using the offline cache of the device's web browser1.

PWAs were introduced from 2016 as an alternative to native (device-specific) applications, with the advantage that they do not require separate bundling or distribution for different platforms. They can be used on a range of different systems, including desktop and mobile devices. Publishing the app to digital distribution systems like the Apple App Store or Google Play is optional.

Because a PWA is delivered in the form of a webpage or website built using common web technologies including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and WebAssembly, it can work on any platform with a PWA-compatible browser. As of 2025, PWA features are supported to varying degrees by Google Chrome, Apple Safari, Firefox for Android/Windows, and Microsoft Edge.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Progressive\_web\_app](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Progressive_web_app)

1OutSystems uses the device’s offline cache to install a light-weight MySQL database engine, allowing apps to store data in offline mode (no internet connection), with capability to synch data between the device and the server once the device comes back online.  PWA apps have access to the device hardware also, like the phone camera.

2. Click on “Sign up” to register yourself with LGB Bank.

| ![A screenshot of a login screenDescription automatically generated][image5]  | ![][image6] |
| :---: | :---: |

When signing up, make sure you use your actual email address and mobile number, so that you will be able to receive notifications.

Also note the “Forgot password?” feature is fully functional.  It will send a One Time Password (OTP) to you via SMU and Email.  When you login using the OTP, you will be routed to a “Reset Password” page to set your permanent password.

3. Login.  Then you will see the Home page.

| ![][image7]  | ![][image8] |
| ----- | :---: |

As a new LGB Bank customer, you are given 1 deposit account with a balance of $10,000 and 1 credit card with a limit of $20,000.  You are also given 5,000 loyalty points (1 point \= $1)

4. Go to the Profile page and note down your assigned “Customer ID”.  Note it is exactly 10 characters in length.  You will need this ID to test your composite service.

| ![][image9]  |  |
| ----- | :---: |

5. At this time, you may also explore the Accounts page where you can see your Account numbers and balances, and the Insurance page where you can see that you don’t have any insurance policies yet.

| ![][image10]  | ![][image11] |
| ----- | :---: |

Task 2	Create a new Module \- IssueInsurancePolicy

1. Create a **New Application**.

![A white square with a white arrowDescription automatically generated][image12]

2. **From Scratch**

![A screenshot of a screenDescription automatically generated][image13]

3. As a **Service**

![A screenshot of a web pageDescription automatically generated][image14]

4. Name the new Application as “IS213 LGB Case Study Services”

![][image15]

5. Create a new Module as “LGB\_IssueInsurancePolicy” of type “Service”.

![][image16]

6. Open the newly created application.

![][image17]

Task 3	Setup Site Properties

1. Under the **Data** tab, Right-Click on Site Properties, and add the following:

![][image18]

| ![][image19] | Name IS213LGB\_ApiKey Text Default Value "7f052295-d9d6-485b-b39a-07f0b9d3286f" (Note: must include the quotation marks) |
| :---- | :---- |
|  ![][image20] | Name RabbitMQHostname Text Default Value "active-white-bear-01.rmq6.cloudamqp.com" (Note: must **include the quotation marks**)  |
|  ![][image21] | Name RabbitMQPassword Text Default Value: "VRMsjW\_248ItCPA3gSjNFl51HfiO1Dt9" (Note: must **include the quotation marks**)  |
| ![][image22] | Name RabbitMQPort Integer Default Value 5672  |
|  ![][image23] | Name RabbitMQUsername Text Default Value: "ntxsydfp" (Note: must **include the quotation marks**)  |
|   ![][image24] | Name RabbitMQVirtualHost Text Default Value: "ntxsydfp" (Note: must **include the quotation marks**) |

Note: 

Site Properties have “Application Scope” and are stored on the server.  Use these to set global parameters that apply to the entire application.  Site Properties are appropriately used to set parameters that might periodically change, e.g., API keys.

Client Variables on the other hand are stored in the device’s browser memory, and are only applied to the user who is logged in.  Use these to set user variables that might be used across different web pages.  An appropriate use would be to store the users “Customer ID” as in the case with this application.

Task 4	Consume REST APIs

1. Under the **Logic** tab, Right-Click on User Exception, and add a new exception called “RESTException”.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image25]

2. In this section you will consume 3 atomic services, 1 composite service, and 1 wrapper service.  The table below shows the name of the service and its swagger URL.

| Service | Swagger URL |
| :---- | :---- |
| Insurance Account (Atomic) | [https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/LGB\_InsuranceAccount/rest/InsuranceAccount/swagger.json](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/LGB_InsuranceAccount/rest/InsuranceAccount/swagger.json)  |
| Insurance Policy (Atomic) | [https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/INSCO\_InsurancePolicy/rest/InsurancePolicy/swagger.json](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/INSCO_InsurancePolicy/rest/InsurancePolicy/swagger.json)  |
| Make Payment (Composite) | [https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/LGB\_MakePayment/rest/MakePayment/swagger.json](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/LGB_MakePayment/rest/MakePayment/swagger.json)  |
| Party (Atomic) | [https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/SMULab\_Party/rest/Party/swagger.json](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/SMULab_Party/rest/Party/swagger.json)  |
| Rabbit MQ (Wrapper) | [https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/SMULab\_RabbitMQ/rest/RabbitMQ/swagger.json](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/SMULab_RabbitMQ/rest/RabbitMQ/swagger.json)  |

Repeat the following steps to consume each of the above 5 services:

1) Under the **Logic** tab under the folder called Integrations, Right-Click on REST and select Consume REST API….

2) Select Add multiple methods.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image26]

3) Copy-n-paste the swagger URL from the above table into the field below and click Add methods.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image27]

4) Click Select all then Finish.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image28]

5) Repeat the above steps for the remaining 4 services listed in the table.  
     
     
     
     
 


6) Under the Logic tab, you should now see the following REST APIs consumed.  
   

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image29]

7) Under the Data tab, note that the associated data structures are also imported.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image30]

Task 5	Setup Data Structures

1. Under the Data tab, manually create 5 more Structures and their Attributes shown as follows:

![][image31]

1) ContactDetails

| ![][image32]  | ![][image33] |
| :---- | :---- |

2) ErrorStructure  
   

| ![][image34] For the “Text List” data type, choose “List…” first and then select “Text”  | ![][image35] |
| :---- | :---- |

   

3) EventDetails

| ![][image36]  | ![][image37] |
| :---- | :---- |
| ![][image38]  |  |

   

   

4) IssueInsurancePolicyRequest  
   

| ![][image39]  | ![][image40] |
| :---- | :---- |
| ![][image41]  | ![][image42] |
| ![][image43]  | ![][image44] |
| ![][image45]  | ![][image46] |
| ![][image47]  | ![][image48]  |

   

   

   

   

   

   

   

 


   

5) IssueInsurancePolicyResponse

| ![][image49]  |  |
| :---- | :---- |

Note:

Data Structures are an abstraction of a JSON schema.  Schemas define the structure of JSON data, e.g., the names of JSON objects, and the data types of the JSON object values.  JSON arrays are also supported, implemented as a List of a Data Structure.

Also note in this example, the “Name in JSON” field is formatted in camelCase whereby the first letter of each word is capitalized except for the first word which is lower case, as in “insuranceType”.  This format is best practice for API request and response fields that are passed in the message body.  Conversely, the best practice for parameters passed in the request URL is that the first letter of the first word is capitalized, as in “CustomerId”.  Best practices are not strictly enforced.

Task 6	Add Request/Response Handlers

1. Under the Logic tab, click on the Party API and enable OnBeforeRequest and OnAfterResponse handlers.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image50]

Note:

OnBeforeRequest handlers are used to modify the request before it is sent.  This is a convenient place to add request headers, in this case the API key required by the API.  You can also do things like replace the domain name in the base URL of the request, for example to implement dynamic routing to different URL endpoints (the auto-grader for this lab uses this mechanism). 

2. Double-click the Party OnBeforeRequest handler to open the logic flow. Add a ListAppend component (drag from (System) under Server Actions) and fill in:

List:	Request.Headers

Name:	"X-Contacts-Key"

Value: Site.IS213LGB\_ApiKey

To add Name and Value:

![][image51] 

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image52]

3. Add logic to the OnAfterResponse handler under Party.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image53]

| ![][image54]  | ![][image55]  |
| :---- | :---- |
| ![][image56]  |  |

4. Repeat the previous step to add the OnAfterResponse handler for all the APIs shown below (except for IssueInsurancePolicy, which will be built later).  You need to enable both handlers, but you only need to add logic to the OnAfterResponse handlers.  You can copy and paste the logic from the Party API.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image57]

Note:

* OnAfterResponse handlers are used to modify the response from an API before it is passed back to the frontend.  In the case shown above, if an error is returned, it places the error string into an Error Structure and raises an exception to be caught by the frontend to be displayed to the user.  
* Click the “Publish” button in OutSystems to save your logic flow immediately after resolving any errors as changes are not auto‑saved if you don’t “publish.”

Task 7	Add Server Action – PublishPolicyEvent

1. Under the Logic tab, Right-Click on Server Actions (make sure to select the folder containing (System), NOT Service Actions), and add “PublishPolicyEvent”, and add 5 Input Parameters (InsuranceType, InsurancePolicyId, CustomerId, BeneficiaryId, Action) and 3 Local Variables (ContactDetails, EventDetails, Narrative) under it.  This Server Action publishes a “Policy Event” via RabbitMQ.

There already exists a Subscriber to the event called “LGB\_Notification”, which sends an Email and an SMS to inform the user that the insurance policy has been issued.

![][image58]

| ![][image59]  | ![][image60] |
| :---- | :---- |
| ![][image61]  | ![][image62] |
| ![][image63]  | ![][image64] |
| ![][image65]  | ![][image66] |

2. Add the Logic flow as shown below. To add a needed server action, may click on the middle of a right flow arrow and search for the needed action in the popup dialog box.

![][image67]

| ![][image68]  | ![][image69] |
| :---- | :---- |
| ![][image70] Copy-n-paste Narrative value from below: GetOrganization.Response.organization.organizationName \+ " " \+InsuranceType+ " insurance policy " \+ InsurancePolicyId \+ " " \+Action+ " for " \+ GetPerson.Response.person.displayName  | ![][image71]  |
| ![][image72]  | ![][image73]  |
| ![][image74]  |   |

Note: when setting the "Publish" action, we need to expand the "Request" field first, but the "+" sign may not be visible. Nevertheless, just move your mouse over the corner location, the "+" sign should show up. The same when setting "RabbitMQConnection".  Note: The “+” sign will only show up if the input field is empty. 

![][image75]

Task 8	Expose REST API – IssueInsurancePolicy

1. Under the Logic tab under the folder called Integrations, Right-Click on REST and select Expose REST API.

2. Expose a new REST API called “IssueInsurancePolicy” and add an HTTP POST method of the same name.

![][image76]

Also, add an Input Parameter (Request) and an Output Parameter (Response) for the method as shown:

| ![][image77] Also, change "Received In" from URL to Body | ![][image78] |
| :---- | :---- |

3. Add the logic flow as shown below.

![A screenshot of a computer screenDescription automatically generated][image79]

Note, this implements the composite service explained in the lecture slides.

![][image80]

Drag and drop from the APIs we consumed earlier to construct the logic flow. For example:

.![][image81]

| ![][image82]  |
| :---- |
| ![][image83]  |
| ![][image84]  |
| ![][image85]  |
| ![][image86]  |
| ![][image87]  |

4. Publish the Module.

![][image88]

5. Right-Click on the IssueInsurancePolicy REST API and click on Open Documentation.

![][image89]

![][image90]

6. Copy and paste the IssueInsurancePolicy Request URL into Postman.  Copy-n-paste the Example Value into Postman, and set values as follows:

{

  "customerId": "your customer ID",  		🡨 see Task 1 Step 4

  "paymentMethod": "Deposit\_Account",

  "paymentAccount": "your deposit account ID",   	🡨 see Task 1 Step 5

  "paymentAccountCurrency": "SGD",

  "beneficiaryId": "1000000049",			🡨 INSCO’s customer ID	

  "beneficiaryAccount": "8000000012",		🡨 INSCO’s deposit account

  "insurancePlanId": "2000000004",		🡨 Travel Bronze Plan \- monthly premium

  "insuranceType": "Travel",

  "insuranceProposalId": "3000000001",		🡨 use this for testing

  "insurancePremium": 10.19			🡨 small value for testing

}

Then click \[Send\].

![][image91]

Note:

If you repeat this step using the same input parameters, then the status message returned will say {“status”: “renewed”} because it is a premium payment on an existing policy.

Task 9	Test complete solution using Mobile app

1. Go to the Insurance page and click Add insurance policy.  

Then select Type, Coverage, and Payment options.  The selected Insurance plan will be displayed in the middle panel.

Next, select your Payment Method.  Finally, click Add.

| ![][image92]  | ![][image93] |
| ----- | :---: |

2. You will receive a notification via SMS and Email.  
   1. The email may be marked as Junk.  Please check your Junk emails folder, or review quarantined messages at https://security.microsoft.com/quarantine.

![A close up of a emailDescription automatically generated][image94]

![A screen shot of a messageDescription automatically generated][image95]

3. Verify that your Insurance Policy has been added, and that your account balance has been reduced.

| ![][image96]  | ![][image97] |
| :---- | :---: |

4. Try adding more insurance policies.  There are 18 different combinations of Type, Coverage, and Payment.

Task 10	Extra Stuff

1. Create a new request in Postman to see what Relationships have been created for you under the Party service.

[https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/SMULab\_Party/rest/Party/GetRelationships?partyId=your\_customer\_id](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/SMULab_Party/rest/Party/GetRelationships?partyId=your_customer_id)

The value for the partyId field in the URL should be your Customer ID (See Task 1 Step 3). 

Also, the Authorization should be set to API Key and be filled as follows:

Key \= 	X-Contacts-Key  
Value \= 	7f052295-d9d6-485b-b39a-07f0b9d3286f

![][image98]

2. Create a new Deposit Account for yourself.

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image99]

[https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/LGB\_DepositAccount/rest/DepositAccount/CreateDepositAccount](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/LGB_DepositAccount/rest/DepositAccount/CreateDepositAccount)

{

  "customerId": "your\_customer\_ID",

  "currency": "SGD",

  "initialBalance": 15000

}

1) In the Mobile app, verify that your new account has been added.   
      
   Note: The App won’t know that you called this API using postman, so you will need to log out and login again to see the new account.  If the feature to add accounts were to be built into the App, then you would not need to log out and login to see it.  
     
2) Try adding insurance using your new deposit account.

3. Launch SMULab Utilities to discover all the LGB and INSCO API documentation 

[https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/SMULabUtilities/Home](https://smuedu-dev.outsystemsenterprise.com/SMULabUtilities/Home)

![][image100]

Troubleshooting Guide

Here is a link to the troubleshooting guide:

[https://docs.google.com/document/d/1x-U\_hR9UBCGXdlzDOzevMrWfO9QndGkGZsAlFaXtCFs/edit?usp=drive\_link](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1x-U_hR9UBCGXdlzDOzevMrWfO9QndGkGZsAlFaXtCFs/edit?usp=drive_link)

Thank You
